style_desc: Benefit-led — opens with a concrete 2026 benefit/saving, then qualifies.
output:
**TITLE:** Save $2,100 on Prescription Costs in 2026

**OPENING:**
"Hello, my name is [Agent Name] and I'm calling from [Licensed Insurance Agency]. We're reaching out to help you understand your Medicare benefits for 2026."

**PITCH:**
"With the new 2026 Part D plan, many of our members are saving up to $2,100 on their prescription costs. This means that after reaching the annual out-of-pocket cap, your medication will be fully covered by the plan for the rest of the year."

**QUALIFY:**
"Are you currently enrolled in Medicare Part A and Part B?"

**OBJECTION HANDLING:**

* If "not interested": "I completely understand. We're happy to help answer any questions you have about your Medicare benefits. May I take a minute to discuss?"
* If "is this a scam / who are you": "I'm a licensed insurance agent working with [Licensed Insurance Agency]. We're not affiliated with the government, and we're here to provide honest guidance on your Medicare options."
* If "I'm busy": "No problem at all. May I take just one minute of your time to discuss how we can help you save on prescription costs in 2026?"

**TRANSFER:**
"Would you like me to transfer you to a licensed specialist who can discuss your individual plan options and answer any questions you may have? They'll be happy to help."

**CLOSE (not interested):**
"Thank you for your time, and I wish you all the best with your Medicare benefits. Please know that we're here if you need us in the future. [Honor Do-Not-Call by including company name and a message like 'We respect your privacy']"

Note: The script is designed to be compliant with CMS marketing rules and emphasizes the 2026 Part D out-of-pocket cap savings as the primary benefit. It also confirms Part A & B eligibility and provides an opportunity for interested individuals to speak with a licensed specialist.
